name: Daemon Duration Emission

tasks.title: Add duration_ms to execute stage_complete records
tasks.description: Add duration_ms to execute stage_complete records
tasks.body: In internal/daemon/iteration.go, record time.Now() immediately before the stage_start log call (~line 102). After the invocation completes, compute elapsed := time.Since(startTime) and add "duration_ms": elapsed.Milliseconds() to the stage_complete log map (~line 126). The field must be an integer (int64), not a float. Preserve all existing fields in the map.
acceptance_criteria: stage_complete records in iteration.go include a duration_ms integer field

tasks.title: Add duration_ms to intake stage_complete records
tasks.description: Add duration_ms to intake stage_complete records
tasks.body: In internal/daemon/stages.go, record time.Now() immediately before each intake invocation's stage_start log call (~line 179). After invocation, compute elapsed := time.Since(startTime) and add "duration_ms": elapsed.Milliseconds() to the stage_complete log map (~line 233). The timing should be per-item (each inbox item gets its own duration), not total batch time.
acceptance_criteria: stage_complete records for intake include a duration_ms integer field

tasks.title: Add duration_ms to planning_complete records
tasks.body: In internal/daemon/planning.go, record time.Now() immediately before the planning_start log call (~line 199). After invocation, compute elapsed := time.Since(startTime) and add "duration_ms": elapsed.Milliseconds() to the planning_complete log map (~line 214). The field name is duration_ms (not planning_duration_ms) for consistency across all completion record types.
acceptance_criteria: planning_complete records include a duration_ms integer field

tasks.title: Add unit tests for duration_ms emission
tasks.description: Add unit tests for duration_ms emission
tasks.body: Write or extend tests to verify that stage_complete and planning_complete log records contain a duration_ms field with a non-negative integer value. The tests should verify: (1) duration_ms is present in stage_complete records from iteration.go, (2) duration_ms is present in stage_complete records from stages.go, (3) duration_ms is present in planning_complete records. Use the existing test patterns in the daemon package. If a test logger or mock logger captures log records, assert the field exists and is >= 0.
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify duration_ms is present and non-negative in stage_complete records
Tests verify duration_ms is present and non-negative in planning_complete records

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Daemon Duration Emission is complete and correct

audit_enrichment: Verify duration_ms appears in all three emission sites (iteration.go, stages.go, planning.go) and is computed as elapsed.Milliseconds() from a time.Now() captured before the invocation

breadcrumbs.text: Added duration_ms (int64) field to stage_complete log records in iteration.go. Captures time.Now() before stage_start, computes elapsed via time.Since after invocation completes.

task-0001.objective: Add duration_ms integer field to stage_complete log records in iteration.go
task-0001.what_happened: Added stageStartTime := time.Now() before the stage_start log call at line 102, then computed stageDuration := time.Since(stageStartTime) after invocation and added duration_ms: stageDuration.Milliseconds() to the stage_complete map. All existing fields preserved.
went_well: Minimal two-line change, no new imports needed since time was already imported
action_items: Downstream tasks should add similar timing to renderer and other log sites
